eng_incidents.title: API gateway 502 errors - checkout service degraded
eng_incidents.description: Customers receiving 502 errors on checkout requests. Error rate spiked from 0.1% to 18% at 14:32 UTC. Cart creation succeeding but payment processing failing intermittently.
eng_incidents.resolution: Reverted checkout-service to v2.4.1. Root cause was a connection pool exhaustion bug introduced in v2.4.2.
eng_incidents.root_cause: Connection pool limit set too low in v2.4.2 configuration change - max_connections set to 10 instead of 100.

eng_incidents.title: Nightly batch job timeout - analytics pipeline delayed
eng_incidents.description: Nightly ETL job exceeded 6-hour timeout, causing analytics dashboards to show stale data from prior day.
eng_incidents.resolution: Increased job timeout to 8 hours. Identified slow query on events table - added index on created_at column.
eng_incidents.root_cause: Missing index on high-cardinality events table caused full table scan on 200M row dataset.

eng_incidents.title: Auth service latency spike - login times elevated
eng_incidents.description: Average login latency jumped from 180ms to 2.4s. No errors, but user experience severely impacted during peak traffic hours.
eng_incidents.resolution: Scaled auth service replicas from 3 to 8. Latency returned to normal within 4 minutes of scale-out.
eng_incidents.root_cause: Insufficient autoscaling headroom during traffic spike from marketing email campaign launch.

eng_incidents.title: Redis cache flush - cache miss storm on product catalog
eng_incidents.description: Ops team flushed wrong Redis instance in production. Product catalog cache emptied, causing 100% cache miss rate and database overload.
eng_incidents.resolution: Emergency read replicas brought online to absorb database load. Cache repopulated over 22 minutes via warm-up job.
eng_incidents.root_cause: Ops runbook did not clearly distinguish staging vs production Redis instance names. Human error during maintenance.

eng_deployments.change_summary: Fix connection pool limit bug, add payment retry logic, upgrade Stripe SDK to v14
Add autoscaling policy for peak traffic, raise HPA max replicas to 20, tune resource limits
Add index on events.created_at, increase job timeout to 8h, add progress checkpointing
Major version upgrade - new rate limiting engine, request tracing integration, updated auth middleware

eng_services.name: checkout-service
eng_services.description: Handles cart creation, payment processing, and order confirmation

eng_services.name: auth-service
eng_services.description: Authentication and authorization - login, token issuance, session management

eng_services.name: analytics-pipeline
eng_services.description: Nightly ETL pipeline for analytics dashboard data aggregation

eng_services.name: product-catalog
eng_services.description: Product metadata, inventory counts, and search index serving

eng_runbooks.title: Checkout Service - Connection Pool Exhaustion
eng_runbooks.steps: 1. Check error rate on checkout-service dashboard. 2. If error rate >5%, confirm connection pool metrics in Datadog. 3. Scale service replicas immediately: kubectl scale deployment checkout-service --replicas=10. 4. If rate does not drop, roll back to previous version. 5. Open incident and page on-call.
eng_runbooks.author: dana.park@company.com

eng_runbooks.title: Auth Service - Scale Out During Traffic Spike
eng_runbooks.steps: 1. Confirm auth latency is above 500ms in Datadog. 2. Check current replica count: kubectl get hpa auth-service. 3. Manually override HPA: kubectl scale deployment auth-service --replicas=15. 4. Monitor latency over 5 minutes. 5. Once below 200ms, allow HPA to resume control.
eng_runbooks.author: priya.mehta@company.com

eng_runbooks.title: Redis Cache - Emergency Warm-Up
eng_runbooks.steps: 1. Confirm cache miss rate spike on Redis dashboard. 2. Identify affected services and alert their owners. 3. Run cache warm-up job: kubectl create job --from=cronjob/cache-warmup cache-warmup-emergency. 4. Monitor job progress. 5. If product catalog affected, scale read replicas: kubectl scale statefulset postgres-read --replicas=5.
eng_runbooks.author: alex.torres@company.com

eng_tasks.title: Implement distributed tracing for checkout-service
eng_tasks.description: Integrate OpenTelemetry to trace requests from API gateway through checkout-service to Stripe and back
eng_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Every checkout request produces a complete trace visible in Jaeger. P99 latency impact <5ms.

eng_tasks.title: Upgrade auth-service JWT library to v9
eng_tasks.description: Current JWT library has a known vulnerability in token parsing. Upgrade to v9 which patches CVE-2026-1234.
eng_tasks.acceptance_criteria: All tests pass. No regression in login latency. Security scan clean.

eng_tasks.title: Add index to analytics_events.created_at column
eng_tasks.description: Missing index causing full table scan on 200M row table during nightly ETL. Index adds ~10GB but reduces query from 4h to 8min.
eng_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Nightly ETL completes within 2 hours. No degradation to write performance during business hours.

eng_tasks.title: Design rate limiting architecture for API gateway v4
eng_tasks.description: Define rate limiting rules, storage backend (Redis vs in-memory), and failure mode behavior for the v4 gateway upgrade
eng_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Architecture doc approved by platform lead. Burst handling tested under simulated load.

eng_tasks.title: Write Redis flush runbook with environment safeguards
eng_tasks.description: After the production Redis flush incident, create a runbook that requires explicit environment confirmation before any flush command
eng_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Runbook reviewed by ops team. Includes two-step confirmation procedure. Added to incident response library.

eng_sprints.name: Sprint 21
eng_sprints.goal: Resolve post-incident tech debt from April incidents and close all open security vulnerabilities

eng_sprints.name: Sprint 22
eng_sprints.goal: Improve observability and prepare API gateway v4 for staging deployment

eng_risks.title: API gateway v4 migration may cause downstream breaking changes
eng_risks.description: The v4 gateway introduces new auth middleware behavior. Services relying on header passthrough may break without code changes.
eng_risks.mitigation_plan: Run v4 in parallel on staging for 2 weeks. Audit all downstream services for header dependencies. Provide migration guide 2 weeks before rollout.

eng_risks.title: Analytics pipeline outage during Q2 close if ETL does not stabilize
eng_risks.description: Finance depends on analytics dashboards for Q2 close on June 30. If the nightly job continues to fail, finance will be blocked.
eng_risks.mitigation_plan: Index added in sprint 21. Monitor job duration for 3 weeks. If duration remains above 4 hours, add incremental processing to avoid full table scans.
